app.name: VideoApp
app.tagline: Private messaging, sharp calls, and communities built for daily use.
app.description: VideoApp is a WhatsApp-style communication stack with a public website, Android app, Node.js backend, MongoDB, Socket.io delivery, release management, and an admin dashboard in one repository.

hero.eyebrow: Secure communication for teams, families, and creators
hero.headline: One private place for chat, calls, status, and communities.
hero.body: Built as a production-ready full-stack product with OTP auth, live releases, real-time delivery, moderation tooling, and a premium public website that can actually run your launch.

seo.title: VideoApp | Private Chat, Calls, Communities, and Android Downloads
seo.description: Launch a secure WhatsApp-style experience with live APK delivery, moderation tools, mobile updates, and a public website wired to the backend.

features.title: Private chat
features.copy: Fast one-to-one conversations with delivery states, reply threads, archived chats, and offline recovery.

features.title: Voice call
features.copy: Launch voice sessions instantly from chat or call history with live call state updates across devices.

features.title: Video call
features.copy: Prepared for camera-first conversations with room signaling, mute controls, and polished in-call UX.

features.title: Status
features.copy: Share text, photo, and video updates with viewer tracking and familiar 24-hour freshness.

features.title: Communities
features.copy: Organize announcement groups, nested groups, and invite links under a single community hub.

features.title: Moderation
features.copy: Reports, bans, announcements, download metrics, release management, and log visibility from one admin panel.

features.title: File sharing
features.copy: Send documents, photos, videos, and voice notes with safer upload handling and stricter file policies.

features.title: Release management
features.copy: Publish APK metadata, changelogs, forced update thresholds, and live website downloads without leaving the repo.

features.title: Multi-device ready
features.copy: Browser-friendly landing and admin surfaces keep the product ready for broader multi-device expansion.

screenshots.title: Chats that stay readable under pressure
screenshots.subtitle: Pinned conversations, rich previews, reply context, and fast delivery updates.
lines: It is live on the website now.
Perfect. I am joining the community next.

screenshots.title: Status built for quick stories
screenshots.subtitle: Text, media, view counts, and 24-hour freshness in one stream.

screenshots.title: Call flows that feel immediate
screenshots.subtitle: Voice and video state, active call controls, and post-call history.

screenshots.title: Communities that hold everything together
screenshots.subtitle: Announcement channels, invite links, and clear group structure.

testimonials.quote: The release panel and live download tracking turned our launch page into an actual operations surface, not just marketing wallpaper.
testimonials.name: Priya Menon

testimonials.quote: Reports, bans, announcements, and logs all being visible in one place made moderation dramatically less chaotic.
testimonials.name: Arjun Patel

testimonials.quote: Users could move from the website to install in seconds. The QR flow and changelog reduced a lot of drop-off.
testimonials.name: Nina Fernandes

faq.question: How do users get the latest Android build?
faq.answer: The website reads the current release metadata and serves the tracked APK download flow with version, changelog, and force-update thresholds.

faq.question: Is the backend protected for public launch?
faq.answer: Yes. The production setup includes refresh-token sessions, rate limiting, stricter upload validation, CORS allowlists, Helmet headers, moderation rules, and structured error logging.

faq.question: What can admins do from the dashboard?
faq.answer: Admins can log in securely, review users and reports, ban or unban accounts, broadcast announcements, publish releases, inspect download counts, and review recent error logs.

faq.question: Can the stack be deployed on separate services?
faq.answer: Yes. The repository is prepared for static website hosting, containerized backend deployment, MongoDB Atlas, and custom-domain HTTPS with provider-managed or reverse-proxy SSL.

updates.title: Phase two launch readiness
updates.summary: Production deployment, admin controls, release management, and security hardening landed across the whole stack.

updates.title: Admin dashboard goes live
updates.summary: User moderation, report review, broadcast announcements, error logs, and APK management are now available from the web admin surface.

updates.title: Android release pipeline prepared
updates.summary: The project now carries structured release metadata, changelogs, build numbers, and update thresholds that power both the website and mobile app.
